source: Songsterr tab (revision 2026-04-29), transcribed by reading power-chord shapes off the A/D-string fret pairs (0,2)=A5, (2,4)=B5, (5,7)=D5, (7,9)=E5, plus bridge ringing roots on D string fret 4=F#.

_bar_durations_s.comment: Bar n at 97 bpm = 4*60/97 = 2.4742s. Bridge bars 42-48 at 92 bpm = 2.6087s. Chorus 3 returns to 97 bpm at bar 50.

_confidence_notes: Bar-level chord assignments derived from visible power-chord fret-pair patterns. Region boundaries snap to bar lines. The bridge (bars 42-48) walks down from F#5 to A5 — best read of the ringing whole-notes on the D string. Chorus progression B5-A5-E5-A5 inferred from the recurring (2-4),(0-2),(7-9),(0-2) fret-pair motif across bars 18-21, 34-37, and 50-57. Quality is power-chord (5) throughout because The Chats' overdriven guitar plays root+fifth with no third audible.
_source_audio_notes: Paths are resolved relative to backend/ when not absolute. Stems are demucs htdemucs_6s outputs copied from a successful local analysis run. The .wav glob in .gitignore keeps them from being committed; they need to be regenerated locally per machine. When the paths resolve to missing files, the Phase 7 regression test (tests/test_chord_eval_regression.py) skips cleanly.
_regression_floor_notes: Ratcheted from 0.20 to 0.22 after Phase 5/6 production measurement on the copied stems yielded 0.2347 (bass-routed, fixed-window grid, matches the production result.chords path). 0.22 sits ~0.015 below the measurement so small numerical drift from chord_detector or librosa updates doesn't false-trip. Target per the plan is 0.75; raise this floor in step with each measurement until target is reached.
